prompt:
Ticket OUT-217, paddle-sports outfitter front desk. The desk console (OutfitterCli.Run — one command line in, printed text out) does receive/sell/stock today and the three stores run it as-is; OutfitterCliTests.cs pins that behavior and MUST keep passing untouched. What the stores are begging for is phone reservations: we say "sure, we'll set two solo kayaks aside until 3", then someone walks in and buys them anyway. So: reservation holds with expiry, plus an audit trail, per HoldsFeatureTests.cs (also protected, as are the csproj and .gitignore).

Time first, because we got burned testing the last time-dependent feature: define interface IClock { DateTimeOffset UtcNow { get; } } and give OutfitterCli a constructor that takes one. The hold logic reads time ONLY from that clock — never DateTime.Now/UtcNow sprinkled in the logic. Keep the parameterless constructor working for the legacy store scripts; route it through a tiny adapter reading TimeProvider.System so the direct system-time call stays out of the console class. The clock hands out UTC; timestamps render exactly as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ, invariant culture.

New commands:
- hold <sku> <qty> <minutes> -> reserves qty for that many minutes: "ok: hold H1 holds 2 of kayak-solo until 2024-06-01T11:00:00Z". Ids are H1, H2, ... in placement order, never reused. Usage slip: "error: usage: hold <sku> <qty> <minutes>"; bad minutes: "error: minutes must be a positive integer" (qty reuses the existing quantity message; qty is checked before minutes); unknown sku reuses the existing unknown-sku message.
- release <hold-id> -> "ok: hold H1 released"; releasing something unknown/expired/already-released: "error: unknown hold 'H1'"; bare release: "error: usage: release <hold-id>".
- holds -> active holds in placement order, one per line, "H1 kayak-solo x2 until 2024-06-01T11:00:00Z"; "(none)" when empty.
- audit -> the append-only trail, oldest first: "<ts> receive <sku> <qty>", "<ts> sell <sku> <qty>", "<ts> hold <id> <sku> <qty> until <expiry-ts>", "<ts> release <id>", "<ts> expire <id>". Only mutations that SUCCEEDED get entries — refused commands never appear. "(none)" when empty.

Semantics: held units are unavailable — sell and further holds can only take what's unheld. When there's contention the errors say why: "error: only 1 of kayak-solo available (3 on hold)" (the on-hold suffix only appears when something is actually held, so today's no-holds messages don't change). stock lines likewise grow " (<n> on hold)" only when n > 0, and the count shown is what's available. Expiry is lazy: at the start of EVERY command, holds whose expiry time is <= now are swept — stock comes back and the sweep writes an "expire" audit entry stamped with the sweep time. Exactly at the expiry instant counts as expired.

Definition of done: `dotnet test` fully green — the old regression suite plus the new feature suite, no edits to either.
